div1.entryFree:
Untrue, 1) not true, contrary to fact: if it appear not plain and prove u. All's V, 3, 318.
Substantively: my most true mind thus maketh mine u. Sonn. 113, 14 (some M. Edd. makes mine eye u.). Adverbially: that you for love speak well of me u. 72, 10 (contrary to truth).
2) faithless, not to be trusted: this man's u. Compl. 169. to make us thus u. LLL V, 2, 473. he that steeps his safety in true blood shall find but bloody safety and u. John III, 4, 148. when to my good lord I prove u. Cymb. I, 5, 86.
